title: fix(polly): kimi-code model, glm fallback, 402 bail, crawl4ai fixes, rnet→scrapling→crawl4ai cascade
author: Itachi-1824
pr_body_excerpt:
Small set of fixes for Polly:

- Switch main model to `kimi-code`, `glm` as fallback for non-balance errors
- HTTP 402 now bails immediately (balance is global, no point retrying)
- Tool loop: inject escalating guidance into context so the AI can self-correct instead of looping forever
- Embeddings skip re-indexing on restart if the repo HEAD hasn't changed
- Discord link formatting rules in system prompt (suppress preview bloat, embed named refs)
- Fixed a couple crawl4ai API deprecations
- Removed the broken web tool (model no longer exists upstream)

gist.headline: Polly hits the books
gist.blurb: Polly just got a major brain upgrade. She's now powered by kimi-code, learns from her own tool loops, and formats Discord links without the preview bloat.
gist.summary: Upgraded Polly's primary model to kimi-code with a glm fallback, added self-correcting tool loops, and implemented embedding caching to skip re-indexing when the repo HEAD is unchanged.
gist.impact: Users will notice smarter code responses and cleaner Discord links, while the bot itself restarts faster and recovers gracefully from tool errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny graduation cap or reading a glowing book/code manual, representing a 'brain upgrade' and new models. A retro monitor displays code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny graduation cap, reading a glowing spellbook of code. A chunky retro monitor nearby displays a lime green (#ecf874) terminal with 'kimi-code' text. Soft pastel lavender and mint gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
